commit.message:
docs(vocab+wording): unit is a 'governance mechanism' not a 'control'; scope codegen claim; soften 'you don't need it'

Vocabulary sweep (unit = mechanism; keep 'control' only as the A.21 observe-and-guard CONCEPT):
- '## Related controls' -> '## Related mechanisms' across all 53 entries + catalog.py parser/SECTIONS
- INDEX: '# Control census' -> '# Mechanism census', 11 'Control' column headers -> 'Mechanism', count prose
- catalog.py: census <th>, INDEX-count regex, STAT_VOCAB, landing strings (kept internal data-census key)
- README/CLAUDE/quick-start/figure/role-READMEs: unit-sense 'control(s)' -> 'mechanism(s)'
- KEPT: README 'Control' guardrail-kind concept, the construction-mechanism-vs-enforced-control contrast,
  development-workflow.html 'controls' (concept + CSS), the 'governance-doc-controls' family dir name

Wording:
- codegen claim scoped: models GOVERN the codebase; a limited slice (config, docs, IPC contracts) is
  generated from them — was overstated as 'the codebase is generated from'
- writing-style: new rule — avoid the 'the X is a Y' copula except very rarely
- quick-start: drop 'the repo is a plugin marketplace' aside; reference only the paper at the end;
  soften 'a mechanism you can't attach to a failure is one you MIGHT not need yet' (+ allow anticipated
  failures — ex-ante governance)

docs(writing-style): ban the "X is a Y" copula (except rarely); drop the "repo is a plugin marketplace" aside from quick-start

- CLAUDE.md Writing style: new bullet — prefer the verb for what X does over the equative copula
- quick-start.md Path A: strike the parenthetical (itself an instance of the copula)

test(html): validate with html-validate (canonical) — delete the hand-rolled balance check

'Valid HTML' isn't ours to define (A.9 genre-check). Replaces the bespoke _Balance/_VOID/_CONTAINERS
well-formedness parser with html-validate (npm, offline, pinned via npm ci, run through npx --no-install)
as a Tier-2 check — needs_run-gated on HTML changes, skip-if-absent, same posture as axe. No new runtime
(chose it over the W3C vnu's Java dep).

It immediately caught real bugs the balance check + axe both missed:
- an unescaped '&' in the hand-templated landing (no-raw-characters),
- two aria-label-misuse a11y errors in the workflow figure (fixed via role=img),
- a deprecated iframe scrolling= attr, and a single-quoted charset.
Configured the pure-style rules off (void-style/no-inline-style/doctype-style/long-title — all valid
HTML, our choices); kept the validity core. CI --full runs it; setup.sh/npm ci install it.

sec(render): close two markdown stored-XSS vectors via a single stdlib escape seam + href scheme-allowlist STRIDE found two renderer injection paths the CI/exposure scan missed (PoC'd): - V2 (auto-exec): abbreviation-cite display text [[slug|]] was inserted raw by _abbr_link — now escaped. This also fixes a latent invalid-HTML bug (a stray unescaped '&'). - V1 (click): markdown link [x](javascript:/data:...) passed the scheme through — _md_link_rewrite now allowlists http/https/mailto/#/relative and neutralizes any other scheme to '#'. Systematic, not point-patches (A.2.7 one seam): all HTML escaping now routes through _esc = the stdlib html.escape (canonical, not hand-rolled); _attr = html.escape(quote=True). Guards: check_escape_seam (validate) forbids a hand-rolled escape-chain anywhere; check_link_schemes (validate) fails loudly on a disallowed scheme; a render-safety pin test (Tier-1) fixes both PoCs to escaped output. Also scopes the axe test HTTP server to the built site (404s .git/node_modules/dotfiles) instead of serving repo ROOT.

ci(security): harden Pages workflow — job-scoped perms, pinned tooling + SHA-pinned actions, minimal artifact

Threat model: public static-site repo; the Pages deploy workflow is the only privileged automation.
Hardening (see DEVELOP.md § Security analysis):
- job-scoped least privilege: build job (runs axe) holds no write caps; pages:write + id-token:write live only on deploy job
- pinned test tooling: committed package-lock.json (integrity-hashed tree); npm ci; axe via 'npx --no-install' (no runtime registry fetch)
- SHA-pin every 'uses:' action (immutable commit, not movable tag)
- assemble _site/ excluding .git/node_modules/.github (minimal deploy tree)
- scanners (html_files + orphan gate) exclude node_modules — surfaced once npm ci made it present in CI
No served page changed (output-neutral).

catalogue: make CLAUDE.md repo-only (link → GitHub blob) + add BLOCKING orphan-page reachability gate to build

- NOSERVE CLAUDE.md; git rm CLAUDE.html (only inbound link repointed to the GitHub blob)
- check_orphan_pages(): post-build scan flags any rendered .html nothing links to (the DEVELOP.html class); wired BLOCKING into build + deploy
- check_links: skip external http(s) .md URLs

chore: inline declared stats (drop root stats.json) + NOSERVE DEVELOP (remove orphan DEVELOP.html)

- stats.json (bad name + root clutter) -> DECLARED_STATS constant in catalog.py; file deleted, one
  obvious edit site, same derived-from-catalogue fill
- DEVELOP.md is a dev doc nothing links to -> NOSERVE + remove the orphan DEVELOP.html page
- README.html kept: README.md renders and is linked from all three role pages as 'the catalogue'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: untrack + gitignore internal HANDOFF docs (kept local, not shipped)

HANDOFF.md + HANDOFF-catalogue-agent.md are agent continuity notes, not public artifacts. Untracked
(local copies kept) and gitignored via /HANDOFF*.md so they leave the public repo and never ship again.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: PRIVACY.md is raw-md only (NOSERVE), not a catalogue page — remove PRIVACY.html

The site is about the catalogue, not the skill; the skill's privacy policy lives as a plain md in
the repo (link via the GitHub blob), not a rendered Pages page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add PRIVACY.md (no code exec, no network, no data — guidance only) for submission URL

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): run the suite --full in CI — post-push HEAD==origin/main, so incremental gating would skip Tier 2

Second-order effect of needs_run gating: in CI the pushed commit IS origin/main, so 'changed vs origin'
is empty and axe/claude would auto-skip every run — silently gutting the a11y backstop for any push that
bypassed the local deploy gate. Adds --full (force run-all, reusing the no-baseline path) and wires the
Pages workflow to it. Two-tier model: CI = authoritative --full (makes origin green); local predeploy =
incremental fast-check that trusts CI's baseline.

feat(test): needs_run incremental gating — skip a check when its declared inputs are unchanged vs origin/main

Adds a coherent per-check interface instead of an axe-specific hack: each Check may declare
needs_run(changed)->bool over the set of paths changed since origin/main. When false the check SKIPs
('inputs unchanged') because its verdict is a pure function of those inputs. Wired for the two expensive
Tier-2 checks — axe (any served .html changed) and 'claude plugin validate' (any plugin/ or .claude-plugin/
changed); Tier-1 stays always-run (sub-second). No origin/main baseline -> run everything (fail-SAFE: a
stale/missing baseline over-runs, never skips a real change). Turns the common prose/skill commit from a
~90s suite into ~1s.

revert plugin name -> self-governance (plugin==skill) + local-only SUBMISSION.md

- plugin/marketplace name back to self-governance (invocation self-governance:self-governance,
  install self-governance@agent-governance-mechanisms); user prefers plugin==skill
- SUBMISSION.md: paste-ready community-marketplace blurb, gitignored + NOSERVE (local reference,
  not published, not committed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(plugin): rename plugin self-governance -> agent-governance (skill name unchanged)

Cleaner invocation before community-marketplace submission: agent-governance:self-governance instead
of the doubled self-governance:self-governance. Touches marketplace.json + plugin.json plugin name and
the quick-start install line; skill name + dir + source path unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(quick-start): lead Path A with one-command marketplace install; copy-paste as fallback

The repo is already a plugin marketplace (.claude-plugin/marketplace.json) — surface it:
/plugin marketplace add davisjam/agent-governance-mechanisms + /plugin install
self-governance@agent-governance-mechanisms. 'No marketplace needed' was stale; copy-paste is now
the labeled fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(starter): apply partner's critical lens across Part A

- A.2.6: 'permanent' -> 'ongoing' two-paths tax (a shim's tax lasts until you remove it — the
  same overclaim class the partner fixed in A.2.11)
- reflex 'load-bearing' -> concrete (A.2.5 core affordance, A.3.5 essential elements, A.4.3 central
  discipline); kept A.4.1 'surface load-bearing decisions' (the autonomy principle's name)
- genericize source-specific names: one-canonical-walker rule -> one-walker-per-tree disciplines;
  '[LINT] in addition to [FIX]' -> 'a lint that prevents the class, not just a one-off fix'
- drop 'the author's own repos' from the A.4 attribution footer

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
docs(method): sharpen A.2.11 — essential = irreducible problem+structure (not just domain rules); removal stays gone only once a control pins it

docs(starter+style): break-up-blocks style rule + apply to CLAUDE-starter

- CLAUDE Writing style: new 'Break up blocks; prefer bullets' rule (paragraphs 2-4 sentences, lists
  not comma-runs, bold lead-ins)
- starter intro: de-blocked into frame + context + bulleted four-groups w/ intra-doc anchor refs;
  drop 'carried across the author's repos'
- A.1.1: fix 'control' overload -> 'mechanism' (governance = architecture OR control); trim the
  three-target roll-call to 'e.g.'; add catalogue URL for looking up each concept
- A.2.11 essence-vs-accident: genericize DocAble example -> business requirements / regulatory
  compliance; bulletize (essential=budget, accidental=attack)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
docs(skill): surface A.1.2 (map, don't re-teach) as reflex #8 + de-bold exemplar foundations per writing style

docs(method): re-org Part A under the governance view (Architecture/Controls as the spine) + add failure-map linchpin

Reorganizes the CLAUDE-starter's Part A from a flat A.1-A.24 list into a
governance-centric hierarchy — A.1 the governance view, A.2 Architecture
(prevent), A.3 Controls (catch), A.4 operating the method — so the standard
engineering playbook is presented AS governance mechanisms, which is the repo's
thesis. Adds A.1.2 'Complement competence — map the playbook to the failures it
governs' as the linchpin (flaky/DRY/SRP/reinvention/silent-degradation mapped to
architecture-or-control, not re-taught). Folds single-responsibility into
class-vs-module (A.2.4) and the canonical-exemplar reservoirs (Apache/CNCF/
Postgres/Redis/Kafka/...) into genre-check (A.2.8). Renumbers all internal
(-> A.X) cross-refs, SKILL.md pointers, and the landing-page count (no adopters
yet, so renumber is free). principles.md regenerates from Part A on build.

docs(landing): Starter CLAUDE.md card guides 'have Claude fold it into your CLAUDE.md'

Closes the one website touchpoint that handed the starter over cold (no 'have Claude look at it'
framing). Repo/quick-start/skill already carried the fold-the-method guidance; the landing's direct
download card now does too, pointing at Quick start for the full prompts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(bootstrap): surface folding the method into an existing CLAUDE.md — quick-start section + README pointer + SKILL capability note

Adds a 'Fold the method into your own CLAUDE.md' section to quick-start (before/after-install prompts to integrate the AI-First Engineering stance into an existing governance doc, not a greenfield rewrite), a README pointer, and a SKILL.md capability note so the skill knows it can propose adopt/adapt/skip edits against the user's CLAUDE.md.

docs(style): writing-quality batch from two-auditor read + capture lessons in CLAUDE

- 11 entry fixes: cut property-tests sell + 2 'load-bearing' keeps + f10 restatement/qualifiers +
  content-validator/pdf-model/semantic qualifiers + drift/query 'the whole point' + walkers self-aside +
  flagship 'One line:' -> plain closer (light-touch)
- CLAUDE Writing style: cross-section restatement, no-superlatives + no-self-comment, causal-vs-intensifier
  qualifier nuance (reliably / 'the whole point' added; keep 'precisely because')

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(claude): add 'What is a mechanism?' — 3 bars incl. examples-must-instantiate-it (rel. audit)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(distinctness): sharpen semantic-lints to per-source; config-field lives only in coherence-lints

The config-field-in-sample lint is relational (record <-> sample), so it belongs to coherence-lints.
Removed all 5 config-field references from semantic-lints (Intent, Motivation, why-not x2, Known uses),
replacing with per-source examples (Console.WriteLine, banned API). The two entries no longer share the
batching example, so per-source vs cross-source reads cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(style): tighten office-models Intent (2 sentences) + drop View-4 takeaway echo

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(style): B3 sweep — system-models pattern-note (executable-source-of-truth x6 domains)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(style): B2 portable-first Intents (4 entries) + B3 mediators pattern-note canary + B4 carving principle

- B2: pdf-model/office-models/standards-rule-engine/a11y-prefix Intents lead with the portable
  pattern + '(our instance: …)'; rest already complied
- B3 canary: mediators INDEX descriptor names the one resource-mediator pattern + the 3 cardinalities
- B4: CLAUDE content model states the dedicated-bundles / cross-cutting-stands-alone carving rule;
  audit clean (no merges — f10/drift-parity are cross-cutting, correctly separate)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(catalog): stats meta-file + data-census fill + no-raw-stats guard

- stats.json: declared facts (LOC 430, case-study weeks 12) not derivable from entries
- _stats(): single source = derived (controls/families/roles/enforcement 46·4·3) merged over declared
- _sync_figure_census fills all data-census spans from _stats; migrate figure's hardcoded
  53/46/4/3 + '280 KLOC' (->430) into spans
- check_no_raw_stats: validate gate forbidding a stat literal (KLOC/controls/families/weeks)
  outside a data-census span; prose numbers (N=8) exempt by vocabulary
- verified: build idempotent (2nd build empty diff), guard catches injected '999 controls'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(catalog): census-count drift guard + INDEX fix (Agent 22, gov-doc 6) + writing backlog + Intent style rule

- check_summary_counts: validate gate asserting INDEX prose role/total numbers == parsed

- fix the stale footer numbers; WRITING-BACKLOG.md (NOSERVE); CLAUDE Writing style: instance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(style): active voice in standards-rule-engine (RuleWalkers produce findings)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(style): apply Writing style to hand-authored figure + landing + SKILL.md

  ('why every project should', 'the enabling substrate')
- catalog.py landing string + SKILL.md: cut redundant 'load-bearing'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(style): cut reflex adverbs (critically/crucially/the crux) per Writing style

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(style): active voice + de-sell pass per CLAUDE Writing style

- 13 'is what X' -> active 'X does' across entries (kept one purpose-idiom)
- models-bridge: rename 'Why this is the crux' -> 'Why this matters'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: cut 'load-bearing' tic 20->2 (active voice) + add Writing style to CLAUDE.md

- reword 18 'load-bearing' uses to specific, active-voice phrasing (X trusts Y);
  keep 2 where it's the right term (staged-deploy-gates, executable-source-of-truth)
- CLAUDE.md: new Writing style section (Hemingway — active voice, say-it-once,
  concrete words, cut qualifiers, describe don't sell)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: trim verbosity — quick-start starters list, models-bridge de-essay, flagship restatement + dangling refs

- quick-start: 'Ready-made starters' run-on -> bulleted list; drop re-link sentence
- models-bridge/README: drift thesis stated once (was 3-4x); remove universal over-claim
- dynamic-context-injection: cut 4x->1x restatement in 'Why it's not just'; drop dangling talks-and-notes/ + analysis/episodes/ paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(readme): trim redundant caution — one tighter 'why not' + neutral tone

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
docs(quick-start): tighten intro to two bullets; bullet the skill's install-time properties
docs(quick-start): lead with the skill (Path A batteries-included), DIY as Path B; drop not-yet-live marketplace pitch

docs: add HANDOFF-catalogue-agent — continuity doc for the catalogue-agent

Detailed handoff distinct from the skill-agent's territory: ownership split +
shared-surface coordination, the systems built (abstractions glossary, downloads
starters, the hand-authored figure + its gates, naming/licensing rules), the full
validate-gate inventory, editing conventions to keep gates green, open items, and
locked decisions. Internal (NOSERVE); scrubbed of the banned PDF-library name so
check_banned_terms stays green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: stop naming the PDF library (licensing) + guard + fix View-3 bridge colour

- Say "canonical PDF library" / "raw-PDF-library ban-lint" instead of naming the
  vendor library or its itext-* lint names, across pdf-model, office-models,
  product/README, and the figure (itext-ban -> PDF-lib ban).
- New validate gate check_banned_terms: fails (pre-commit + predeploy) if the
  banned term is named anywhere in the sources. Scans sources, not generated HTML.
- Figure: add "blue = models-bridge" to the colour legend so View 3's blue
  query-surface node (a bridge control) is no longer an unexplained colour.

validate 0; zero iText anywhere except the ban's own definition in catalog.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(figure): make View 3 wiring slugs clickable

Every control named in the "wiring by relationship" edges now links to its writeup
(subtle dotted-underline, matching the grid). Informal counterparts that aren't
catalogue entries (cap-lint, itext-ban, openxml-ban, scope-doc, file-addr. rules)
stay as plain text. validate link-integrity confirms all targets resolve.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: don't imply the source setting is max governance — "adapt", not "adapt down"

The catalogue's origin operation is high-intensity but not the ceiling; a larger fleet
or a higher-stakes/regulated domain may rightly need MORE. Drop the "adapt down"
directionality (README + A.24) and note explicitly that scaling up is valid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(figure): un-compress View 3 "wiring by relationship" — group per relationship

The one-row-per-relationship layout ("label — item · item · item") read as a
compressed wall and hid which legend items were used. Now each relationship is
its own group: a labelled header (with the relationship's definition pulled in
as a sub-label so it's self-explanatory) over a vertical list of its edges, one
per line, in a responsive grid. Taller and legible over dense.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: name the tower-of-governance risk + calibrate governance to scale

The upside was sold on three surfaces with no counterweight; the guards were buried
inside principles. Make the primary failure mode explicit and teach right-sizing the
whole apparatus (not just individual fixes):

- README: new "Why you might NOT want to use this" section — governance has a cost;
  small operations need little; the tower-of-governance failure mode + the adverse
  selection (governance-seekers over-build); descriptive-not-prescriptive, adapt down.
- Part A: new principle A.24 "Proportion governance to the operation" — match intensity
  to pain, small operation -> light touch, remove controls that stop earning. Broader
  than A.22 (per-fix right-sizing): this is per-operation calibration. Flows into the
  skill's principles.md + the starter.
- SKILL.md: audit mode gauges scale and sizes the WHOLE plan to it (a light operation
  gets a light plan); a Notes bullet names the tower risk and says skip even when the
  user asks for more.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(validate): figure legend-usage gate — every legend element must be used

check_figure now also asserts that every encoding the legend declares (the
counterpart/enabler/consumer/layer/bridge relationships + the Soft/Soft·Hard
enforcement styles) is actually used in the diagram body, not just declared —
a legend that over-promises fails validate (→ pre-commit + predeploy).

Currently passes: counterpart 8· enabler 3· consumer 4· bridge 5· layer 1·
Soft 4· S·H 2· Hard 53. (Measurement, not assumption.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(skill): ship the full CLAUDE-starter download + add A.23 testing strategies

Q1 — Part B is now a portable "write your own" scaffold (no source-project specifics),
so ship the whole starter governance doc, as a *download* (not folded into principles.md):
- claude-md-rule-index entry gains an "Adopt it ->" handoff to downloads/CLAUDE-starter.md,
  so the existing vendor step pulls the full Part A + Part B into reference/downloads/.
  principles.md stays = Part A (the operating stance). Also turns the earlier phantom
  provenance path into a real, shipped file.

Q2 — testing strategies are a general *means* of enforcing governance, not a mechanism,
and the product-role test entries are excluded from the skill, so the skill had none:
- new Part A principle A.23 (terse, a few words each — Claude knows these): property-based,
  doc-driven, schema-driven, fuzz, state-machine coverage, user-journey, dynamics-aimed.
  Regenerates into principles.md; SKILL.md ambient stance gains a one-line pointer so the
  agent reaches for the right *kind* of test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(figure)+feat(validate): every control clickable + a "slug without a link" gate

Figure review:
- Remove "regression suite" from the shipped-artifact flow — it validates the tool,
  it's not a gate the shipped document passes through.
- View 3 (soft↔hard lattice) Hard column: the ~46-name text dump is now fully
  clickable (subtle dotted-underline links, inheriting the existing text colour).
- Surfaced two controls that were missing from the lattice (operational-playbooks →
  Soft, epic-&-design-templates → Soft·Hard); corrected the stale counts (51→53,
  Soft 3→4, Soft·Hard 2→3).
- The 8 View-1 relationship chips ("PdfModel ═ itext-ban", …) now link the control
  name, imitating the existing chip a11y pattern (link text + title; decorative
  ═/──▶ symbols left as plain spans as before).

New gate (catalog.py check_figure, runs in validate → blocks pre-commit + predeploy):
- link integrity — every relative .html link in the figure resolves;
- coverage — every control is linked at least once (no slug clickable nowhere);
- no orphan node — a chip/lat-node styled clickable must carry a link.

validate: 0 issues (coverage confirmed all 53 linked; caught the 8 orphans above).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(skill): correct the bundled-INDEX count rewrite (source wraps "fully\nwritten")

The prior regex required "fully written" contiguous, but the source line wraps across
a newline, so it never matched. Allow \s+ and match to the sentence period.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(skill): clear the two cosmetic issues the independent review found

- principles.md provenance comment cited `downloads/CLAUDE-starter.md`, a build-source
  not shipped in the bundle → reword to name the source without a phantom bundle path.
- bundled reference/INDEX.md inherited the full catalogue's "All 53 entries are fully
  written" while the bundle ships 33 (product excluded) → rewrite to the bundled count
  at assembly time, computed from the included roles.

Both were flagged by a fresh-brain review of the built skill; both are one-line copy
edits in the generated output; neither affected installability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(figure): write out "MIS", remove leftover flagship/star, link every chip

Governance-map figure review fixes:
- "MIS" (uncommon acronym) -> "merge-train-batching" chips + "independent-set
  batching" heading; tooltips already spell out "maximum independent set".
- Remove the half-removed star system: drop the `.chip.flag` gold-outline CSS,
  the `flag` class from all 12 chips, and the "gold outline = family flagship (★)"
  legend line.
- The two unlinked chips (NetworkPolicy·wiring, boundary/seam lints) now link to
  their controls (model-driven-codegen, drift-parity-gates) — no chip looks
  clickable without being clickable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(tests): factor catalog_tests into a driver + tests/ package (one module per kind)

catalog_tests.py had grown to 9 checks across 4 kinds + the runner in one file. Split
(behavior-identical, suite stays 9/9):
- catalog_tests.py -> the DRIVER: CHECKS registry + tiered fail-fast runner + main; still
  the entry point (`catalog.py test` runs it).
- tests/common.py  -> shared paths, PASS/FAIL/SKIP, rel/slug/run/html_files/free_port.
- tests/markdown.py, tests/html.py, tests/skill.py, tests/external.py -> one kind each.

Adding a check is now: a function in the right kind-module + one line in CHECKS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: rename "control-map figure" -> "governance map" everywhere

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: cheap HTML well-formedness gate before axe + fail-fast Tier-1 -> Tier-2

- new Tier-1 "html: well-formed (balanced containers)" check: stdlib HTMLParser stack
  asserts every built page's container tags (div/section/main/figure/table/pre/svg/...)
  balance and nest. Optional-close elements (p/li/tr) are untracked so valid HTML
  doesn't false-positive. A browser auto-corrects malformed HTML, so axe alone wouldn't
  catch a renderer dropping a close tag; this does, in milliseconds.
- runner now fail-fast: Tier-2 (axe ~88s, claude) runs ONLY if every cheap Tier-1
  check passed. Verified: a broken page fails well-formedness and axe is skipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(README): soften "not browsed by hand" — point humans to the web view first

"Using this repo" now leads with "to read it yourself, use the web view (the pretty
version)", then frames the raw repo as what you hand your agent — rather than telling
readers not to browse it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(README): add "start small + your environment" on-ramp + closing paper link

- "Start small, and make it yours": the catalogue is a census of how far governance
  can go, not a checklist — start with the highest-leverage controls for the failures
  your agents actually make; growing guardrails from real failure IS the method.
- Environment note reframed as a strength, not a concession: the mechanisms are
  patterns not a stack, but the durable part is local-machine productivity BEFORE CI —
  the agent fails fast against the same gates a reviewer would, so defects are caught
  before the push and CI becomes confirmation. "Trustworthy locally, not just in CI."
- Closing line: "To learn more about the experience behind this repository, see the
  Cheap Code, Costly Judgment paper."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: tiered catalogue + skill test suite, wired into deploy + CI (audit-only)

catalog_tests.py — a dependency-tiered suite (run via `catalog.py test`):
  Tier 1 (stdlib, always, hard):
    - markdown schema + .md-link existence (delegates to `catalog.py validate`)
    - markdown #anchor resolution (link anchor must be a heading in the target)
    - html link + anchor resolution over the built site
    - skill structure + manifests, and a bundle-freshness (no-drift) check
  Tier 2 (external, SKIP if absent / FAIL under --strict):
    - axe-core accessibility over representative pages (Node + Chrome)
    - `claude plugin validate` on the plugin + marketplace manifests

Wiring: `catalog.py deploy` and the Pages CI both run the suite (AUDIT-ONLY —
report, don't block) because of a known backlog it already surfaced:
  * 2 broken README cross-anchors (agent/ + models-bridge/ README -> README.md#…)
  * 6 site a11y violations (axe)
Flip to blocking once `catalog.py test` is green.

Deps + docs: package.json (@axe-core/cli, dev-only), setup.sh (installs it),
DEVELOP.md (extend a mechanism / write tests / dependency map). node_modules gitignored.
The catalogue core stays stdlib clone-and-run; Node is only the optional a11y tier.
